artPrompt: A glowing spell-form, half-cast as a knot of crackling cyan energy, is sucked sideways into a wire in-tray and held there suspended in translucent amber stillness, a single ghostly die hovering above awaiting a verdict that never comes. Towering shadowed cabinets rise around it. Sweeping pull into bureaucratic limbo, ivory and teal washes bleeding. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: Pending Further Review
text: Exile target spell. At the beginning of its controller's next end step, they may resubmit it: cast it from exile without paying its mana cost if they roll a six-sided die and get a 6. Otherwise, the matter remains pending and the spell is exiled forever, lost to committee.
flavorText: Status: pending. It has been pending since before you were born.
